response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically broad full-stack engineer with genuine depth in low-latency systems and a long career spanning embedded, web, mobile, and AI tooling. However, the Principal Growth/Experimentation role requires deep, demonstrable expertise in experimentation platforms, A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging, telemetry pipelines, and statistical rollout-risk management — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. The candidate presents as a strong generalist IC rather than a domain specialist in growth systems, and there is no evidence of cross-team platform ownership or the kind of measurable growth-engineering leadership this IC6 role demands.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O)
Broad polyglot skill set (TypeScript, C++, C#, Java, D) covers several of the required languages
Strong software craftsmanship fundamentals including TDD, CI/CD, and developer tooling — signals production quality discipline
concerns: Zero explicit experimentation platform experience: no A/B testing frameworks, feature flagging systems, bias detection, guardrail metrics, or rollout automation mentioned
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure ownership — a core pillar of the role — appears anywhere in the employment history or projects
Seniority gap: titled Senior Software Developer with no demonstrated IC6-level cross-team technical leadership or multi-workstream initiative ownership at scale
